response_raw:
## 1. Rider objective & context

The footage appears to be a point-of-view (POV) clip recorded on a DJI Osmo Action 5 Pro, timestamped 2026. At least two off-road motorcycles are visible at various points, riding through a pine forest on uneven, sloped terrain. A second rider/motorcycle (red, identifiable as a GasGas in frame 3) is visible ahead of or near the camera rider at multiple points.

---

## 2. Rider

- **Frames 1–2:** A rider on a red motorcycle is visible at distance ahead; body position not clearly discernible due to distance and tree obstruction.
- **Frame 3:** A second rider is visible in close-up on the right side — wearing a dark jacket, helmet, and boots; mounted on a red GasGas motorcycle. The rider appears to be stationary or near-stationary.
- **Frames 4–6:** The camera rider's own handlebars and red bodywork are partially visible at the bottom of frame; rider body not visible.
- **Frames 7–8:** Two figures and a red motorcycle are visible at the top of a hill in the distance; detail is not clear.
- **Frames 9–15:** Camera rider's own handlebars and front bodywork visible intermittently; rider body not in view.
- **Final two frames (16–17):** No rider visible in frame. The motorcycle is lying on its side on the ground.

---

## 3. Terrain & environment

- Dense pine forest throughout all frames.
- Ground is dry, brown, covered in pine needles, dead leaves, twigs, and scattered fallen branches and logs.
- The terrain is noticeably sloped — a hillside with visible gradient running diagonally across multiple frames.
- Tree stumps visible, suggesting partial clearing or prior logging activity.
- Some rocks and moss-covered boulders visible on the slope (frames 9–13).
- No defined trail or path is clearly marked; the ground shows faint linear compression marks consistent with prior wheel passes in some frames.
- Overcast sky visible through the tree canopy.
- Some green undergrowth visible lower on the slope in later frames.

---

## 4. Events

- **Frames 1–2:** Camera rider is moving through the forest; a lead rider is visible ahead, ascending or traversing the slope. In frame 2, the lead rider/motorcycle appears to be stopped or fallen near a tree — partially obscured.
- **Frame 3:** A rider with a red GasGas motorcycle is visible stationary on the right side of frame, close to the camera.
- **Frames 4–6:** Camera view shows the terrain ahead — the camera appears to be moving, tracking uphill. Two figures with a motorcycle are visible at distance on the hilltop.
- **Frames 7–8:** Camera view tilts, showing a steep hillside with fallen branches. The camera angle becomes increasingly sideways/tilted.
- **Frames 9–13:** Camera continues to show sloped terrain with fallen branches and a leaning dead tree; the camera angle remains oblique, suggesting the camera-mounted motorcycle is in a tilted or fallen orientation.
- **Frames 14–15:** Camera angle is nearly sideways, looking along the slope; a red motorcycle is barely visible at distance in the trees (frame 15).
- **Frames 16–17:** A red motorcycle is shown lying on its side on the forest floor, with no rider visible in frame. The bike is on the downhill side, leaning against the slope. Multiple body panels, handlebars (Renthal bar ends visible in frame 16), exhaust, and wheels are visible. The bike appears to have come to rest on its side.

---

## 5. Outcome

The camera-mounted red motorcycle is shown on its side on the ground in the final two frames. The bike is visibly lying on the forest floor with no rider in frame. The sequence of tilting camera angles in frames 8–15 is consistent with the camera-mounted bike gradually falling or being laid over. The rider is not visible in the final frames.

---

## 6. Unclear or not visible

- The rider of the camera bike is not visible at any point (camera is mounted on the bike, not a helmet or chest).
- It is not visible whether the rider is on the ground, standing nearby, or out of frame.
- The exact moment of the fall/tip-over is not captured — the camera progressively tilts without showing a sudden impact event.
- The lead rider seen in frames 1–2 is largely obscured by trees; their status after frame 3 is unclear.
- Whether the GasGas in frame 3 and the camera bike are the same or different motorcycles is not definitively determinable (both appear red).
- The specific model/year of the camera bike is not fully legible.
- No audio cues are available from still frames.
- Rider condition after the fall is not visible.
